citations.narrative:
On December 28, 2025, a member of the public was participating in a personal encounter with two otters when he was
bitten on his hand. During the encounter, a facility employee produced food for the otters, which caused the otters to
become excited and vocalize. The guest extended his hand toward one of the otters. The otter turned and bit the guest
between the thumb and index finger of his right hand, leaving four shallow puncture wounds.
Failure to handle animals in a manner that minimizes risk can result in injury or be harmful to the health and well-being of
the animal or the public.
During public exhibitions, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public. To
be corrected from this day, March 10, 2026, forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.131(c)(1) 
   
Handling of animals. 
On December 28, 2025, a member of the public was participating in a personal encounter with two otters when he was 
bitten on his hand. During the encounter, a facility employee produced food for the otters, which caused the otters to 
become excited and vocalize. The guest extended his hand toward one of the otters. The otter turned and bit the guest 
between the thumb and index finger of his right hand, leaving four shallow puncture wounds.  
 
Failure to handle animals in a manner that minimizes risk can result in injury or be harmful to the health and well-being of 
the animal or the public. 
 
During public exhibitions, any animal must be handled so there is minimal risk of harm to the animal and to the public. To 
be corrected from this day, March 10, 2026, forward. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with a facility representative. 
 
 n
